title: Roasted Springy Mexican Dates Sopes
description: Bitter, springy, crowd-pleaser Mexican dish featuring stoned dates, clay-pot-cook, macrobiotic-friendly, form=sopes. A combination of earthy, bitter dates, crispy tortillas, and creamy avocado yields a satisfying and filling meal.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large clay pot, mix together the chopped dates, red onion, cilantro, lime juice, olive oil, garlic, salt, and black pepper.
Place the clay pot in the preheated oven and roast for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Warm the corn tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
In a separate pan, heat a small amount of oil over medium-high heat and cook the tortillas for 30-45 seconds on each side, until crispy and golden brown.
Assemble the sopes by spooning the roasted date mixture onto the tortillas, followed by a dollop of avocado.
Sprinkle fajita-style seasoning on top of the avocado.
Repeat the assembly process for the remaining tortillas and date mixture.
